name: Polie counts regardless of score (gate removed, Story 2.4a) — the SAME inputs as the removed 2.3 polie-bogey-or-better.json, now settled count-only. b1 made a polie while scoring DOUBLE-bogey gross: the 2.3 gate VOIDED it ($10/side); count-only it now COUNTS (-1) -> $5/side. Proves the bogey-or-better gate is gone (FR16: a checked polie always counts). All nets = par so base points are 0; gross is present but UNREAD by polie now.
_contract: Golden HAND-CALC fixture for the F1 polie modifier after Story 2.4a stripped the bogey-or-better gross gate. Polie is now a PURE COUNT claim (identical to sandie): poliePointsA = (# teamA polie boxes) - (# teamB polie boxes), each unit a team point, ALWAYS counts when checked (no eligibility gate). gross is retained on HoleState (reusable, Story 2.5) but polie does not read it. SettlementEdge IR = post-2.1a whole-dollar 1-to-1. Integer cents; fromPlayerId PAYS toPlayerId.
_handCalc:
Teams A={a1,a2} B={b1,b2}, flat $5/pt, polie ON (count-only, no gate). Every net = par => base 0. POLIE (always counts when checked, gross IGNORED):
  H1 (par4): a1 polie => +1.
  H2 (par5): a2 polie => +1.
  H3 (par4): b1 polie => -1 (b1 scored double-bogey gross=6, but the gate is GONE so it COUNTS).
Round polieA = +1+1-1 = +1 => a1=a2=+500c (+$5), b1=b2=-500c. Edges b1->a1 500, b2->a2 500. total 1000c.
CONTRAST vs Story 2.3 (gated): the 2.3 engine VOIDED H3's polie (b1 gross 6 > par+1=5) => polieA=+2 => $10/side. Removing the gate makes b1's polie count => $5/side. This is the money change the strip introduces.
